title: HTTP GET request returns 403 error
symptom: When attempting to retrieve a webpage using the HTTP GET request node, a 403 error is returned. This indicates that access to the resource is forbidden.
not_this_if: The URL is publicly accessible without restrictions
root_cause: A 403 error typically occurs when the server understands the request but refuses to authorize it. This can happen due to missing headers like User-Agent, or if the server has rate-limiting or IP blocking in place after multiple requests.
fix_steps: Add a User-Agent header in the HTTP request settings
Consider adding a Wait Node after each HTTP request to avoid being blocked
Set the On Error option to Continue in the HTTP Node settings
agent_instruction: Add a User-Agent header and implement a Wait Node after each HTTP request.
